forecast-period.text: A weak high pressure system moves across from the west today. A significant low pressure system moves over waters to the south of SA on Monday and Tuesday reaching western Bass Strait Tuesday afternoon. A another low pressure moves over waters to the southeast on Wednesday. A high pressure system follows the low moving across northern parts of SA later in the week.
For latest warnings go to www.bom.gov.au, subscribe to RSS feeds, call 1300 659 210* or listen for warnings on relevant TV and radio broadcasts.
* Calls to 1300 numbers cost around 27.5c incl. GST, higher from mobiles or public phones.

Morning fog patches. Isolated light showers about the southern agricultural area, mainly about windward coasts and ranges. Showers developing about the west during the afternoon. Maximum temperatures below average, grading to above average in the west. West to southwesterly winds, fresh at times about the central and southeastern coasts, tending fresh and gusty north to northeasterly across western districts.

Gusty showers, possible thunderstorms broadly south of a line from Coober Pedy to Marree, tending to patchy rain at times about the agricultural area. Maximum temperatures generally above average. Freshening northerly winds, shifting north to northwesterly ahead of a fresh to strong and gusty westerly change in the west, reaching central districts at night. Damaging winds possible.

Gusty showers. possible thunderstorms and patchy rain about central and southeastern districts. Isolated showers about the south of the pastoral districts and West Coast, though scattered about coastal parts with possible thunderstorms. Maximum temperatures generally above average. A fresh to strong and gusty westerly change extending throughout, damaging winds possible. Winds easing from the west later.

text.p: Showers about the West Coast and agricultural area, possibly reaching the south of the pastoral districts. Maximum temperatures near average. Fresh and gusty westerly winds.

Cumulative rainfall totals to the end of Wednesday: Less than 5 mm about the Pastoral districts south of about Coober Pedy to Marree, increasing to 2 to 10 mm about the northern agricultural area, 5 to 15 mm about the southern agricultural area and increasing to 15 to 30 mm about the Mount Lofty Ranges, Kangaroo Island and the South East, with isolated falls in these areas of 30 to 60 mm.
